Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from...

This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-09T17:29:12.0549646Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T17:27:47.461627637Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58010,\"title\":\"[Identity] Docs: README does not reflect Azure.Core 1.53 type consolidation\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Identity`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57200 by `@m-nash`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57200 moved all 59 public Azure.Identity types into `Azure.Core` and converted `Azure.Identity` into a lightweight `TypeForwardedTo` facade. Starting with version 1.53, credential types such as `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, `ClientSecretCredential`, etc. live in `Azure.Core` and are available to any project referencing `Azure.Core 1.53+` without a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency. A `MigrationGuide.md` was created at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md` and CHANGELOG entries were added to both packages, but the main README was not updated.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; section still instructs all users to `dotnet add package Azure.Identity` with no note that `Azure.Core 1.53+` already includes all credential types.\\n- No mention of the type consolidation or the architectural change anywhere in the README.\\n- No link to `MigrationGuide.md` from the README.\\n- The \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; / \\u0026#34;Prerequisites\\u0026#34; section contains no note that projects already on `Azure.Core 1.53+` can use credentials without adding an explicit `Azure.Identity` PackageReference.\\n- The `Azure.Core` README (`sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`) does not mention that it now bundles the full credential surface (`DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, etc.) starting with 1.53.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34;** section (under `## Getting started`), replace the existing install block with an expanded version that includes a note about `Azure.Core 1.53+`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the Azure Identity client library for .NET with NuGet:\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Starting with `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, all credential types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`) are bundled directly in `Azure.Core`. If your project already targets `Azure.Core` 1.53.0 or later and you do not use any `Azure.Identity`-specific APIs, you can omit the `Azure.Identity` package reference entirely. See the [Migration Guide](./MigrationGuide.md) for upgrade and compatibility details.\\n```\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** section, add a link to the migration guide at the end of the `### Credentials` subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\nFor details on the assembly consolidation introduced in version 1.53 and how to handle `CS0433` conflicts when mixing package versions, see the [Migration Guide](./MigrationGuide.md).\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** bullet list (after the `TokenCredentials` bullet), add a new bullet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Authenticating Azure SDK clients using credential types from Microsoft Entra ID without a separate package dependency. (`DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, and all other credential types from `Azure.Identity` are included directly in `Azure.Core` starting with version 1.53.)\\n```\\n\\nOptionally add an **\\u0026#34;Authentication\\u0026#34;** sub-section in the Examples area pointing to the bundled credential types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticating Service Clients\\n\\nStarting with Azure.Core 1.53, all credential types previously in `Azure.Identity` are available directly:\\n\\n```\\n// No Azure.Identity package reference required for Azure.Core 1.53+\\nvar credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\nvar client = new SecretClient(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` referenced in Step 1 must be backed by a test file:\\n\\n**Test file path:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/tests/Samples/Sample_DefaultCredential.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing System;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Core.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_DefaultCredential\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Sample only\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AuthenticateWithDefaultCredential()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential\\n            // No Azure.Identity package reference required for Azure.Core 1.53+\\n            var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            var client = new SecretClient(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README placeholder location:** in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`, inside the new \\u0026#34;Authentication\\u0026#34; sub-section under `## Examples`, insert:\\n\\n````\\n```C# Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential\\n```\\n````\\n\\nThe `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will inject the actual code between the fences.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order. Only additions are needed; no sections are missing or out of order.\\n\\n`sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — check that the following appear and are complete:\\n1. Getting started ✅ (add install note per Step 1)\\n2. Key concepts ✅ (add migration guide link per Step 1)\\n3. Examples ✅\\n4. Managed identity support ✅\\n5. Troubleshooting ✅\\n6. Next steps ✅\\n7. Contributing ✅\\n\\n`sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — check that the following appear and are complete:\\n1. Getting started ✅\\n2. Key concepts ✅ (add bundled credentials bullet per Step 1)\\n3. Examples ✅ (add authentication section per Step 1)\\n4. Troubleshooting ✅\\n5. Next steps ✅\\n6. Contributing ✅\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n4. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — check `git diff` to confirm README was updated\\n6. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — check `git diff` to confirm README was updated\\n7. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff\\n8. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff\\n9. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n10. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24203535911/agentic_workflow) · ● 534K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58010\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T17:24:26Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T17:24:26Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Examples ✅ (add authentication section per Step 1)\\n4. Troubleshooting ✅\\n5. Next steps ✅\\n6. Contributing ✅\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. all credential methods ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential_opts = new DefaultAzureCredentialOptions\\r\\n{\\r\\n   ExcludeAzureCliCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeAzureDeveloperCliCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeAzurePowerShellCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeEnvironmentCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeInteractiveBrowserCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeManagedIdentityCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeSharedTokenCacheCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeVisualStudioCodeCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeVisualStudioCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeWorkloadIdentityCredential = true\\r\\n};\\r\\n\\r\\n// Enable only the Visual Studio credential ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential_opts.ExcludeVisualStudioCredential = false;\\r\\n\\r\\n// Takes a long time, make sure we do not timeout ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential_opts.CredentialProcessTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds( 30 );\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n// Get Blob service client (works) ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential = new DefaultAzureCredential( azure_credential_opts );\\r\\n\\r\\nm_blob_service_client = new BlobServiceClient( service_uri, azure_credential );\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n// Get Blob container client (works) ...\\r\\n\\r\\nblob_container = m_blob_service_client.GetBlobContainerClient( \\\"container-name\\\" );\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n// Get Blob client (works) ...\\r\\n\\r\\nblob_client = blob_container.GetBlobClient( blob_name );\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n// Check if Blob exists -- KA-POW!, Exeception (above) is raised ...\\r\\n\\r\\nif ( await blob_client.ExistsAsync() ) \\r\\n{\\r\\n\\r\\n}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n### Expected behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nI was expecting the code to work WITHOUT having to explicitly provide a Tenant ID -- At least, that was my understanding after reviewing the documentation.  If misunderstood, please provide a link to any document that indicates a Tenant ID must be explicitly provided.\\r\\n\\r\\nI can (using WORK AROUND 2 shown below) log in via the CLI and run my web app using Visual Studio without issue.  However, when I log in via Visual Studio (TOOLS -\\u003e Options -\\u003e Azure Service Authentication) - the exception will always be raised.   I have (as directed by the documentation) tried to log out and log back in again with no difference in behavior.\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n### Actual behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nAs described above.  The ONLY way I have been able to successfully interact with Azure while using Visual Studio is to:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Include the Tenant ID in an environment variable (see WORK AROUND 1 below).\\r\\n2. Use the Developer CLI credentials (see WORK AROUND 2 below), preferred as I do NOT need to provide a Tenant ID, all I have to do is log in via the CLI -- shouldn't the same behavior be seen when logging in using Visual Studio?\\r\\n\\r\\n**WORK AROUND 1:**\\r\\n\\r\\nExplicitly include the Tenant ID(*) as an environment variable.  Add the \\\"AZURE_TENANT_ID\\\" property to the \\\"environmentVariables\\\" section in the projects \\\"launchSettings.json\\\" file:\\r\\n\\r\\n      \\\"environmentVariables\\\": {\\r\\n         \\\"ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT\\\": \\\"Development\\\",\\r\\n         \\\"AZURE_TENANT_ID\\\": \\\"xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx\\\"\\r\\n      }\\r\\n\\r\\n* To obtain the Tenant ID while using Visual Studio, Click on: TOOLS -\\u003e Command Line -\\u003e Developer Command Prompt to open the command prompt and then enter the following:\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\u003e az login\\r\\n\\r\\nThe following JSON will be displayed after you log in:\\r\\n[\\r\\n  {\\r\\n    ...\\r\\n    \\\"tenantId\\\": \\\"xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx\\\",  \\u003c----- Use the value displayed\\r\\n    ...\\r\\n  }\\r\\n]\\r\\n\\r\\n\\u003e az logout\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n**WORK AROUND 2:**\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\n// Modify the Azure credentials options as follows ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential_opts = new DefaultAzureCredentialOptions\\r\\n{\\r\\n   ExcludeAzureCliCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeAzureDeveloperCliCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeAzurePowerShellCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeEnvironmentCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeInteractiveBrowserCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeManagedIdentityCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeSharedTokenCacheCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeVisualStudioCodeCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeVisualStudioCredential = true,\\r\\n   ExcludeWorkloadIdentityCredential = true\\r\\n};\\r\\n\\r\\n// Enable only the Developer CLI credential ...\\r\\n\\r\\nazure_credential_opts.ExcludeAzureDeveloperCliCredential = false;\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nBEFORE running the app in Visual Studio, Log in to Azure using the CLI:\\r\\n- Open a command prompt\\r\\n- Use the following command to log in to Azure: \\u003e az login\\r\\n- Run the web app locally\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n### Reproduction Steps\\r\\n\\r\\nSee code above.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Environment\\r\\n\\r\\n**VISUAL STUDIO DETAILS:**\\r\\n\\r\\n- Microsoft Visual Studio Community 2022\\r\\n- Version 17.7.6\\r\\n-
